commands.review_summary:
Adds derived OpenClaw provider/onboarding capability tags during package publishing, indexed capabilityTag filtering in the HTTP API and CLI, and a cursor-based reconciliation backfill with docs and tests.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a feature PR, not a reported bug. The relevant verification is after-change CLI/API/backfill proof, which is described but not concretely attached in the PR body.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human maintainer review is needed for the taxonomy and backfill rollout decision, and the contributor must add real behavior proof before merge.

target.title: feat: add contextual onboarding capability tags
target.body:
## Summary

- derive stable provider-kind and onboarding-readiness capability tags from OpenClaw manifest metadata during package publishing
- add exact `--capability-tag <tag>` filtering to `clawhub package explore` in browse and search modes
- add an idempotent, dry-run-capable, cursor-based reconciliation backfill for existing package releases and package/search projections
- document the stable capability-tag contract and backfill invariants

## Derived tag contract

- `capability:model-provider` from non-empty top-level `providers`
- `capability:speech-provider` from non-empty `contracts.speechProviders`
- `capability:web-search-provider` from non-empty `contracts.webSearchProviders`
- `capability:image-generation-provider` from non-empty `contracts.imageGenerationProviders`
- `capability:music-generation-provider` from non-empty `contracts.musicGenerationProviders`
- `setup:text-inference`, `setup:image-generation`, and `setup:music-generation` from valid `providerAuthChoices[].onboardingScopes`
- omitted, empty, or unsupported-only onboarding scopes default to `setup:text-inference`, matching OpenClaw behavior
- existing tags such as `provider:<id>` are preserved; publisher-claimed derived tags are replaced
- non-string provider declarations are ignored; `setup:speech` is intentionally not added

## Backfill design

- dry-run capable, idempotent, bounded, cursor-based, resumable, and scheduled using existing ClawHub maintenance conventions
- reads stored extracted manifests/package metadata with storage fallback
- repairs release capabilities plus latest package, `executesCode`, `latestVersionSummary`, search digest, and capability digest projections
- supports legacy packages that identify the latest release through `tags.latest`
- caps reconciliation pages at four releases for Convex bytes-read safety

No production backfill or production mutation was run.

## Verification

- `bunx vitest run convex/functions.test.ts convex/lib/packageRegistry.test.ts convex/packages.public.test.ts convex/httpApiV1.handlers.test.ts` - 568 passed
- `bun run --cwd packages/clawhub test:src -- src/cli/commands/packages.test.ts` - 80 passed
- `bun run ci:static` - passed
- `bun run ci:unit` - 3175 passed, 1 skipped
- `bun run ci:types-build` - passed
- `bun run ci:packages` - passed
- `bun run docs:smoke` - passed
- local Convex dry-run: scanned 2 releases, patched 0, completed successfully
- live read-only CLI browse and search proofs exercised `--capability-tag capability:model-provider` successfully
- final isolated read-only review: no actionable findings

## Intentional follow-ups

- no `setup:speech` until OpenClaw defines a canonical speech onboarding continuation
- no `setup:web-search`; canonical onboarding scopes remain text/image/music
- skills onboarding-readiness taxonomy remains a separate future contract
- live new-tag queries remain empty until deployment and a deliberate production backfill
target.author: fuller-stack-dev
